reason: The CC param stub lives in MindClient; this spec defines the Agent<->Mind contract call chain — authority for whether a stub change or new param needs a shared-types stub fn.
submit_l4_execution + recommend/validate ladder for CC/RE specialists — the core execution tools the CC->RE flow drives through.
plan_subgraph + specialist_dispatcher routing (cc->cc_subgraph / re->re_subgraph) + _pick_next_planned_step cursor advance — the RE auto-dispatch boundary.
TaskCreatedEvent / FormRequestedEvent / PlanProposedEvent shapes the spec asserts on (execByJobId, form_requested(params) counts).
LabClient.submit_task -> POST /tasks/ contract; the Agent->Lab boundary verified benign across the v1.0.5/v1.1.2a1 pin.
Cross-layer contract rules (Rule 10) — any contract touched during a fix must update spec in the same change set.
Where stub functions + types must live if a new/different MindClient param is needed — types from shared-types, no new Req/Resp bodies.
FE SSE event-dispatcher contract (I-UI-1); the spec gates on form_requested/task_created/task_progress/form_confirmed kinds.
Workspace state machine: plan->params->dispatch->monitor->result lifecycle the cc-re spec walks (duo-panel, params-confirm CTA).
FE<->BE REST/SSE contract (submitFormConfirm payload, decision_id/task_id duo-panel) — the confirm boundary the 4ms-race fix guards.
